title: Cisco ISE Admin Account Lockout, Failed-Login Burst, and Privileged-Role Audit
dataSources: ISE syslog `index=nac sourcetype=cisco:ise:syslog` *Administrative-Operations-Audit* category — codes `60005` (admin login success), `60006` (admin login failed), `60010` (admin account locked), `60050` (admin role added), `60051` (admin role changed), `60052` (admin role removed), `60100` (super-admin role grant). Field: `admin_user`, `admin_role`, `admin_action`, `source_ip`.
spl:
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:syslog" MessageCode IN (60006, 60010, 60050, 60051, 60052, 60100)
| stats count, values(admin_user) as users, values(admin_role) as roles, values(source_ip) as ips by MessageCode
| where (MessageCode=="60006" AND count > 5) OR MessageCode IN (60010, 60050, 60051, 60052, 60100)
description: Detects: (a) Admin login bursts from a single source IP (>5 failures in 5 min, suggesting brute force). (b) Admin account lockouts. (c) Any admin-role change (add/modify/remove). (d) Any super-admin role grant.
value: ISE admin role changes alter who can change network access policy — a high-privilege control. Detecting role changes provides audit evidence for SOX/PCI; brute-force attempts indicate active attacker reconnaissance.
visualization: Admin login failure trend; role-change timeline; super-admin grants log.
knownFalsePositives:
(a) Bulk role changes during planned admin reorg.
(b) Admin accidentally locked out via repeated typo.

compliance.assurance_rationale: PCI 10.2.1.4 — log all changes to admin privileges.
SOX ITGC change management — admin role changes.
HIPAA access establishment & modification — admin role audit.

controlTest.positiveScenario: Trigger 6 failed admin logins from one IP. UC-17.1.71 fires.

grandmaExplanation: ISE admins control who can connect to the network. We watch when admin accounts get locked out, when someone fails login many times, and any change to admin privileges — these are big-deal events.
